mission.title: Install and Configure Tinkerbell
mission.description: Tinkerbell is a CNCF bare metal provisioning engine with workflow capabilities, supporting BMC interactions, DHCP, and hardware auto-discovery. Deploy it to Kubernetes via Helm to automate bare metal server provisioning.

steps.title: Add the Tinkerbell Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Tinkerbell Helm chart repository.

```bash
helm repo add tinkerbell https://tinkerbell.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Tinkerbell using Helm
steps.description:
Install Tinkerbell into a dedicated namespace.

```bash
helm install tinkerbell tinkerbell/tinkerbell --namespace tinkerbell --create-namespace --version 0.23.0
```

> Adjust --version to match your desired release.

steps.title: Verify Tinkerbell pods are running
steps.description:
Check that all Tinkerbell components are running.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n tinkerbell
```

> You should see tink-server, tink-controller, boots (DHCP), and hegel pods.

steps.title: Verify Tinkerbell CRDs
steps.description:
Confirm the Tinkerbell custom resource definitions were installed.

```bash
kubectl get crds | grep tinkerbell
```

> You should see Hardware, Template, and Workflow CRDs.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Tinkerbell
uninstall.description:
Remove Tinkerbell from your cluster.

```bash
helm uninstall tinkerbell -n tinkerbell
```
